title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
How To Check Your PAN Card Status?
PAN card is synonymous with identity in the country and has been in use for a while now. PAN or Permanent Account Number is a mechanism to identify the taxpayers in the country. It is a 10-digit alphanumeric number that is unique and assigned to taxpayers in the country.
This acts as the primary source of all tax-related information for an individual. Though PAN is a number, the PAN Card is a physical card that contains details such as your PAN, name, date of birth, a photograph and the name of your father or spouse. The card holds good as proof of identity or for date of birth.
The concept of PAN was introduced by the government of India in 1972. And under Section 139A of the Indian Tax Act of 1962, it is mandatory to file your taxes. If you do not already have a PAN card, you can apply for one or can apply for a duplicate PAN if you have lost your existing card.
How To Check PAN Application Status
Whether you have applied for a new PAN card or a duplicate PAN card, you can find out your pan card status with a few simple steps. You can check the PAN application status either by calling, by SMS or online.
Call
You can call the call centre number of TIN at +912027218080 and provide your acknowledgement number from your PAN application which is a 15-digit number on the application.
SMS
To check the status of your application via SMS, you can send your 15-digit acknowledgement number to 57575. The status is sent by SMS.
Online
You can also check the status of your card application online. You can check the status of your application via the website of the service provider whose services you have availed to apply for the PAN card. It can be any of the following.
TIN NSDL
UTIITSL
E-Mudhra
Steps To Check PAN Card Status On NDSL Website
Individuals who have applied for their PAN card from NDSL can check the status of their PAN application on the NDSL website. Here are the steps to check the status of the NSDL pan card.
Access the NDSL portal
Select Application Type as ‘Pan-New / Change Request’
Provide your acknowledgement number
Type in the Captcha in the box provided
Click on the submit button
And you should be able to get the status of your NDSL pan card.
Steps To Check PAN Card Status On UTI Website
Taxpayers who have applied for their Pan card using the UTI website need to keep their application coupon number handy. Here are the steps to get online status.
Access the UTIITSL portal
Enter your application coupon number if it is a new application and your PAN number if it is a duplicate application
Type in the Captcha in the box provided
Click on the submit button to view the status
Steps To Check PAN Card Status On the E-Mudhra website:
You can also apply for your Pan card through the e-Mudhra website and view the Pan card status as follows.
Access the e-Mudhra website
Enter your application number along with your date of birth
Type in the Captcha in the box provided
Click on the submit button to view the status
What is PAN Acknowledgement Number?
On successful completion of the application for a PAN card, a taxpayer usually receives a 15-digit unique number. This unique code is known as the PAN acknowledgement number. The ones who apply for NSDL Pan online can then use the number to track their PAN card status online.
The usage of the acknowledgement car is not limited to just checking pan card status. You can also use the number to download ePAN card. You can do this within a month of the issuance of a new card or for an updated card as well. And it is applicable to both the NSDL and UTIISL websites.
How To Apply For PAN Card?
You can visit the local PAN agencies of your district to apply for PAN offline. You can apply for a new PAN card or correct information on your existing one or even apply for a duplicate card.
Alternatively, you can apply for NSDL PAN online. You can take the help of any of the three agencies mentioned above. You would need to fill out Form 49A and submit relevant documents.
Conclusion
Not having a PAN card can make things a little bit tricky for you since it is needed for financial transactions. You might end up paying higher taxes as well. Thus, you must apply for a PAN card at the earliest. And if you do so, you can check the status of your application online using the above methods.
Kw: pan card status, nsdl pan card, nsdl pan online

full_pdf_text:
How To Check Your PAN Card Application Status?
PAN card treated as a valid identity proof for most scenarios in India, It is a 10-digit alphanumeric number that is unique and issued by the Income Tax Department. PAN or Permanent Account Number enables the Income tax department to identify/ link all transactions of the PAN holder. These transactions include tax payments, TDS/TCS credits, returns of income, specified transactions, correspondence etc, and so on.
Though PAN is a number, the PAN Card is a physical card that contains details such as your PAN, name, date of birth, a photograph and the name of your father. The card holds good as proof of identity or for date of birth.
The concept of PAN was introduced by the government of India in 1972 and made statutory under section 139 A of the Income Tax Act w.e.f. 1st April 1976. Section 139A of the Income Tax Act, 1961, lays down various conditions under which you are required to obtain a PAN. You are required to mention your PAN in all communications with the Income Tax Department and while entering into specified financial transactions1. If you do not already have a PAN card, you can apply for one or can apply for a duplicate PAN if you have lost your existing card.
How to Apply For PAN Card?
You can visit the local PAN agencies of your district to apply for PAN offline. You may apply for a new PAN card or update correct information on your existing one or even apply for a duplicate card.
Alternatively, you can apply for PAN online through NSDL or UTIITSL. You may take the help of any of the two agencies mentioned above. You would need to fill out Form 49A which is an application for allotment of PAN and submit relevant documents.
What is PAN Acknowledgement Number?
On successful completion of the application for a PAN card, an applicant receives a 15-digit unique number. This unique code is known as the PAN acknowledgement number. The ones who apply for PAN online through NSDL, can then use the acknowledgement number to track their PAN card status online.
The usage of the acknowledgement number is not limited to just checking pan card status. You can also use the number to download e-PAN card. You may do this within a month of the issuance of a new card or for an updated card as well. And it can be used in both the NSDL and UTIISL websites.
How to Check PAN Application Status?
Whether you have applied for a new PAN card or a duplicate PAN card, you can find out your pan card application status with a few simple steps. You can check the PAN card application status either by calling, through SMS or online.
1. Call
You can contact the call centre number of TIN at +912027218080 and provide your acknowledgement number from your PAN application which is a 15-digit number on the application. 2. SMS
To check the status of your PAN card application via SMS, you can send your 15-digit acknowledgement number to 57575. You will receive back an SMS updating the status of your request. 3. Online
You can also check the status of your Pan card application online via the website of the service provider whose services you may have availed to apply for the PAN card. It can be any of the following2:
● Protean ● UTIITSL Steps to Check PAN Card Status On NDSL Website
Individuals who have applied for their PAN card from NDSL can check the status of their PAN application on the NDSL website. Here are the steps to check the status.
1. Access the NDSL portal https://tin.tin.nsdl.com/pantan/StatusTrack.html 2. Select Application Type as ‘Pan-New / Change Request’ 3. Provide your acknowledgement number 4. Type in the Captcha in the box provided 5. Click on the submit button
Steps to Check PAN Card Status On UTI Website
Taxpayers who have applied for their Pan card using the UTI website need to keep their application coupon number handy. Here are the steps3 to get online status.
1. Access the UTIITSL portal https://www.trackpan.utiitsl.com/PANONLINE/#forward 2. Enter your application coupon number if it is a new application and your PAN number if it is a
duplicate application 3. Enter Date of Birth /Incorporation / Agreement / Partnership or Trust Deed / Formation of Body
of Individuals / Association of Persons, as applicable 4. Type in the Captcha in the box provided 5. Click on the submit button to view the status
Conclusion
Not having a PAN card can make things a little bit tricky for you since it is needed for any financial transactions. Thus, you can consider applying for a PAN card at the earliest. And if you do so, you can check the status of your application online using the above methods.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. Tax benefits as per prevailing Income tax laws shall apply. Please check with your tax consultant for eligibility.

pan.htm#:~:text=Section%20139A%20of%20the%20Income%2Dtax%20Act%2C%201961%2C %20prescribes,entering%20into%20specified%20financial%20transactions. 2. https://incometaxindia.gov.in/Pages/tax-services/apply-for-
pan.aspx#:~:text=Online%20application%20can%20be%20made,%E2%80%8B%E2%80%8B% E2%80%8B%E2%80%8B%E2%80%8B). 3. https://www.trackpan.utiitsl.com/PANONLINE/#forward
